summary: Upgrade the native app to MediaPipe Gesture Recognizer, add guarded browser back and thumbs-up feedback, and require a strict OK pose for pinch scrolling.
scope: Use a single-hand Gesture Recognizer runtime while preserving 21 landmarks, handedness, and the existing scroll math.
Trigger browser back only after a stable Victory pose moves physically left, and only for Chrome, Safari, Edge, or Quark.
Recognize Thumb_Up as display-only feedback without clicking a page or changing a like.
Require thumb-index contact plus extended middle, ring, and little fingers before pinch scrolling can start.
Report inference time and live FPS, adapt from 30 FPS to 24 FPS under sustained load, and keep the HUD status above the Dock.
validation: Release app build, Info.plist validation, bundled gesture model check, and ad-hoc codesign verification passed
Packaged Gesture Recognizer reached MediaPipe GPU ready with the local camera/runtime chain
notes: Real-hand acceptance is still required for four-browser back behavior, 60-second inference performance, and two-minute false-trigger rate.
Apple Vision fallback keeps the previous landmark gestures but does not provide Victory or Thumb_Up classification.
macos-v0.3.0 remains the starred stable rollback point and was not moved or overwritten.
